name: Aphids
symptoms: Yellowing and curling of leaves, stunted plant growth, sticky honeydew secretion on leaves and stems
treatment: Spray Imidacloprid 17.8 SL at 0.5ml per litre of water or Dimethoate 30 EC at 1.5ml per litre
prevention: Use resistant varieties, avoid excessive nitrogen fertilization, encourage natural predators like ladybirds

name: Powdery Mildew
symptoms: White powdery coating on leaves, stems and fruits, leaves turn yellow and dry out eventually
treatment: Spray Carbendazim 50 WP at 1g per litre of water or Sulfur 80 WP at 2g per litre
prevention: Ensure proper spacing between plants for air circulation, avoid overhead irrigation, remove infected plant parts

name: Root Rot
symptoms: Wilting of plants despite adequate water, yellowing of lower leaves, dark brown discoloration of roots
treatment: Drench soil with Carbendazim 50 WP at 2g per litre, remove and destroy infected plants
prevention: Ensure proper drainage, avoid waterlogging, use disease-free seeds, practice crop rotation

name: Stem Borer
symptoms: Dead heart in young plants, white ear in older plants, entry holes visible on stem with frass
treatment: Apply Chlorpyrifos 20 EC at 2.5ml per litre of water, use pheromone traps to monitor adult moths
prevention: Remove and destroy crop stubble after harvest, use resistant varieties, timely sowing

name: Bacterial Blight
symptoms: Water-soaked lesions on leaves turning yellow to brown, wilting of leaves, milky bacterial ooze
treatment: Spray Streptomycin Sulphate at 0.5g per litre combined with Copper Oxychloride at 3g per litre
prevention: Use certified disease-free seeds, avoid injuring plants during field operations, maintain field hygiene

name: Whitefly
symptoms: Yellowing of leaves, sticky honeydew deposits, sooty mould growth on leaves, leaf curl in severe cases
treatment: Spray Thiamethoxam 25 WG at 0.3g per litre or Acetamiprid 20 SP at 0.2g per litre
prevention: Use yellow sticky traps, reflective mulches, remove weeds around field, avoid planting near infected fields

name: Late Blight
symptoms: Dark water-soaked lesions on leaves and stems, white fungal growth on underside of leaves, tuber rot
treatment: Spray Mancozeb 75 WP at 2.5g per litre or Metalaxyl MZ 72 WP at 2g per litre every 7 days
prevention: Use certified disease-free seed tubers, avoid overhead irrigation, destroy infected plant debris

name: Mosaic Virus
symptoms: Mosaic pattern of light and dark green on leaves, leaf distortion and curling, stunted plant growth
treatment: No direct cure — remove and destroy infected plants immediately to prevent spread
prevention: Control aphid vectors by spraying Imidacloprid, use virus-free planting material, rogue out infected plants early
